drafts.preamble: Cleanup tasks identified after the 16-step migration plan (see azt_collabd_plan.xml) landed in v1.22.16. Each draft is self-contained; pick whichever is most useful first.

draft.name: android_contentprovider_transport
draft.goal: On Android, prefer an in-process ContentProvider exported by whichever AZT suite app the user has installed, falling back to the existing loopback + autospawn transport when no provider answers. Removes the requirement that the daemon run as a separate Python process on Android, where subprocess.Popen of the bundled p4a interpreter is unreliable.

architecture.transport_layer:
Add a transport-selection layer in azt_collab_client. rpc.call() becomes a thin facade calling pick_transport().call(...). Public client API unchanged.
azt_collab_client/transports/__init__.py — pick_transport() + Transport ABC. azt_collab_client/transports/loopback.py — current rpc.py logic refactored to a class. azt_collab_client/transports/android_cp.py — ContentProvider transport via pyjnius.
architecture.pick_transport_logic: 1. If platform == 'android', try AndroidCPTransport.discover(). 2. Fall back to LoopbackTransport(). 3. Cache the choice. Re-run discovery on ServerUnavailable so a provider that comes/goes is recovered from.

server_side.description: New module azt_collabd/android_cp/ with two parts: - provider.py: Python implementation that reuses the dispatch table currently inside server._Handler, factored out so both the HTTP handler and the provider call into the same code. - service.py: pyjnius shim that registers a Java ContentProvider class. Java side is a thin pass-through for call(method, arg, extras: Bundle) and openFile(uri, mode).
server_side.wire_format:
Single endpoint, all RPCs go through one Bundle exchange. HTTP-style verb prefix: GET / POST. Path: /v1/projects/fra/sync. body key holds JSON-encoded request body. status (int), json (string of JSON response).
Binary streaming for audio: openFile(uri, mode) returns AssetFileDescriptor; wrapper exposes a Python file-like object via os.fdopen(fd.detachFd(), ...).

android_manifest.buildozer_spec_changes: android.permissions = INTERNET, ..., org.azt.AZT_COLLAB_ACCESS android.add_jars = ... # Java glue jar android.manifest.application_element_extras = ... # the <provider> XML
android_manifest.signing_policy: signature protection level means all AZT suite apps must share a signing key to access each other's provider. Document as the suite's release-key policy.
android_manifest.authority_pattern: <package>.aztcollab per APK; discovery probes the well-known suffix.

discovery.description: AndroidCPTransport.discover() iterates installed providers with PackageManager.queryContentProviders, looks for authorities ending in '.aztcollab', and pings each via ContentResolver.call(uri, 'ping', None, None) until one responds.
discovery.caching: Cache the chosen authority in $AZT_HOME/server.json (existing file gains a "transport": "android_cp" field) so subsequent runs don't re-probe. Stale-detect by trying ping; on failure re-probe.

files_summary.new: azt_collab_client/transports/__init__.py azt_collab_client/transports/loopback.py azt_collab_client/transports/android_cp.py azt_collabd/android_cp/__init__.py azt_collabd/android_cp/service.py Java glue module (built via buildozer android.add_src)
files_summary.refactor: azt_collab_client/rpc.py → thin facade calling pick_transport(). azt_collabd/server.py → extract dispatch table into reusable _dispatch(method, path, body) function.
files_summary.update: buildozer.spec, setup_from_nuke.sh (Java build step), azt_collabd_plan.xml (track this draft as completed when done).

test_surface.android_only: tests/step17_provider.sh runs only on Android (skipped elsewhere).
test_surface.desktop_unit: A pyjnius mock or fake-android shim would let us unit-test the transport's encode/decode logic on desktop. ~50 LOC.
test_surface.integration: Real-device smoke: install recorder + sister app from same signing key, kill recorder, sister app calls list_projects and gets the recorder's projects.

open_questions.q: Provider lifetime. Android keeps provider processes alive on demand, but force-stop kills them. Combine with the existing flock guard so a recently force-stopped provider doesn't conflict with a fresh loopback fallback.
Shared signing. Every suite app must share a release key. Decide where the key lives and how it's distributed across maintainers.

Path-segment authorities. If two installed apps both export the provider, discovery picks the first match. Add a preferred_provider setting (an app id) so the user can pin one. v1 can take "first match wins."

Pyjnius Java callback. Implementing ContentProvider directly in Python via pyjnius is fragile across p4a versions. Likely cleaner: ship a 50-line Java stub class compiled into the APK, with the Python end registered via a callback. Decide which path before starting.

draft.effort: 2-3 days focused work. Most cost is the Java glue / buildozer-build cycle and Android-only test paths needing real-device runs.

draft.name: wire_open_server_ui_button
draft.goal: The recorder already has MyApp.open_server_ui() (added step 10 finish), but no button reaches it. Add a single button to CollabScreen that calls it, so users discover the standalone server settings UI from inside the recorder.
draft.placement: The CollabScreen KV layout in main.py ends with the Publish section. Natural spot: a small "Advanced" row directly below it — visible whenever the screen is, not gated by host toggle.
draft.kv_diff: <CollabScreen>: ... BoxLayout: id: publish_section ... BoxLayout: # ←── new size_hint_y: None height: dp(48) spacing: dp(10) padding: [dp(20), dp(8)] Label: text: _tr('Advanced') size_hint_x: None width: dp(80) color: T.TEXT_DIM RecBtn: text: _tr('Open Sync Settings') normal_color: T.BTN_INACTIVE on_release: app.open_server_ui()
draft.styling_notes: Button uses RecBtn (the existing themed button class) and the same color tokens as other secondary actions on this screen.

translation_strings.note: French at minimum; other languages can fall back to English at first.

behavior.on_tap: App.get_running_app().open_server_ui() runs subprocess.Popen of `python -m azt_collabd ui` detached.
behavior.recorder: Stays open; settings UI opens in its own window / process.
behavior.on_failure: _set_log reports the error in the existing log line at the bottom of CollabScreen.

android_branch.description: On Android, subprocess.Popen([sys.executable, '-m', 'azt_collabd', 'ui']) will not work — no separate Python interpreter to fork. Button needs Android-aware logic.
android_branch.code_sketch: def open_server_ui(self): if platform == 'android': # Phase 1: deeplink to the settings provider via Intent # (only works once the sister-app provider draft above is # in place). Until then, set the log to a friendly hint. self._set_log(_tr('Sync settings UI is desktop-only for now.')) return # ...existing subprocess.Popen path...
android_branch.followup: Android path completes once the ContentProvider transport draft lands; before that, the desktop-only message is the right behavior.

draft.risk: Minimal. KV change is additive; no existing layout changes. Worst case: button doesn't appear on a stale build cache — `buildozer android clean` recovers.

files_touched.file: 8-10 LOC of KV inside the CollabScreen block; one small `if platform == 'android'` branch in open_server_ui().

test_surface.manual: Open recorder → Collab → click "Open Sync Settings" → server UI window appears. Verify the recorder still works after.
test_surface.automated: tests/step18_button_wire.sh: ast.parse(main.py) + regex assertion that the new button literal "Open Sync Settings" appears in the KV string. Light, but catches typos.

drafts.ordering: Independent drafts. wire_open_server_ui_button is a quick win and can ship before the larger ContentProvider work. The Android branch in open_server_ui becomes "do something useful" only after android_contentprovider_transport lands; until then it's a desktop-only message.
